content.full_text:
City of
HAWAIIAN GARDENS
H.M. "LENNIE" WAGNER          DOMENIC RUGGERI          ESTHER E. FLORES          KATHLEEN M. NAVEJAS          ROSALIE M. SHER
MAYOR                         MAYOR PRO TEM            COUNCIL MEMBER            COUNCIL MEMBER              COUNCIL MEMBER


                                    September 12, 1990


     Attorney Louisa Menchuca
     Legal Department
     Fair Political Practices Commission
     428 "J" Street, Suite 800
     Sacramento, California 95814

     RE:  City of Hawaiian Gardens
          Request for Informal Assistance

Dear Attorney Menchuca:

     Pursuant to our brief telephone conversation, I am
writing this letter on behalf of the City Council of the
City of Hawaiian Gardens regarding a potential conflict
of interest.

     The City of Hawaiian Gardens is a general law city
and the City Council is composed of five (5) members; the
mayor is elected by the council members.  All zone
changes, conditional use permits and variances are
finally determined by the City Council.

     In August, 1990, at the direction of the City
Council, the Hawaiian Gardens Civic League, Inc. was
incorporated and has a tax exempt status per R&TC 23701f.
The corporate purposes are generally to promote civic,
cultural, business and recreational, especially youth,
activities in the City.  Enclosed is a copy of the
overall goals and objectives as filed with the Secretary
of State.

     It is anticipated that the Civic League will have
charitable fund raisers and solicit donations as its
chief source of revenue.

     By-laws have not yet been adopted, however it is
proposed that the City Council Members, ex officio, would
constitute the Board of Directors of the Civic League.
They would serve without compensation and would receive
absolutely no financial or other material benefit for
serving as Board Members.

     The conflict of interest issue arises under the
following scenarios:

                                    1

21815 PIONEER BOULEVARD, HAWAIIAN GARDENS CA 90716 • TELEPHONE: (213) 420-2641  FAX: 496-3708

1.     The City is the site of a bingo operation that
generates considerable funds.  The bingo game is operated
by a non-profit Foundation.

          It is foreseeable and anticipated that the
Foundation will be a donor to the Civic League and a
material contributor as a source of revenue.

          Issue:  In the event the Foundation, in the
future, appears before the City Council for a zone
change, conditional use permit, variance, or other
entitlement, would the Council Members have a conflict
of interest if, while serving as the Civic League Board of
Directors  the League received donations from the
Foundation?   Would such an issue arise as to all donors
who contribute Two Hundred Fifty Dollars ($250.00), or
more, in twelve (12) months?

2.  The Chairman of the Foundation that operates the
bingo game owns, in partnership, substantial property in
the City of Hawaiian Gardens.  It is anticipated and
foreseeable that, in his individual capacity, he will be
seeking development entitlements from the City that could
involve zone changes, variances, and conditional use
permits.  Such entitlements must be approved or denied by
the City Council.  Would the City Council be faced with a
conflict since he is applying in an individual capacity
as a landowner, he is also known to be and is serving as
also the Chairman of the Board of Directors of the
Foundation?   (The Foundation has no interest in the
subject property.)

          In the event it is determined that a conflict
exists, and in such event the by-laws of the Civic League
Corporation were to provide that only 1 or 2 council
members were to serve on the Board, couldn't they declare
a conflict and disqualify themselves on such matters as
may come before the City Council?

     Please contact me directly if I can provide further
information.  I can be reached at (213) 630-5913.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        MAURICE F. O'SHEA
                                        City Attorney
                                        City of Hawaiian Gardens


cc:  Hawaiian Gardens City Council
MFO/gac

                                        2

HAWAIIAN GARDENS CIVIC LEAGUE, INC.

PROPOSED BUDGET - FISCAL YEAR 1990/1991


MISSION STATEMENT:

     The purpose of the Hawaiian Gardens Civic League,
Inc. is to serve in developing and promoting programs,
services, and facilities which will enhance the quality
of life for Hawaiian Gardens residents, allowing them to
successfully pursue quality (active or passive) leisure
time experiences.

OVERALL GOALS AND OBJECTIVES:

     1.  To provide services to Hawaiian Gardens
residents and to assist in improving the manner
of doing business in the public leisure
time/recreation industry.

     2.  To develop an organizational structure that
will reflect the goals and objectives of the
Board of Directors and the membership it
serves.

     3.  To promote youth activities and programs.

     4.  To establish policy for the organization.

     5.  To establish short and long range goals and
objectives for the residents and its youth.

     6.  To approve the annual organization budget.

     7.  To provide leadership for membership
development programs at the local neighborhoods
on special interest level.

     8.  To develop strategies for fund raising through
special events, sales programs, membership
drives, grants, etc.

     9.  Develop and approve appointment of all
committee and task forces.

     10. Identify a member of the City's executive staff
to fill the role of Department/City liaison and
organizational administrator for the Hawaiian
Gardens Civic League, Inc.

     11. Look at bingo as a way to generate revenue.
